steps.text: Institute procedures to reduce possible worker exposure, such as use of squeeze cages, working only with anesthetized animals, or other appropriate practices.
Maintain a logbook or other means of documenting the date and time of all persons entering and leaving the animal facility.
Ensure personnel enter and exit the animal facility through the clothing change and shower rooms except during emergencies.
Remove all personal clothing and jewelry (except eyeglasses) in the outer clothing change room.
Use animal facility clothing, including undergarments, pants, shirts, jumpsuits, shoes, and gloves, as appropriate.
Take a personal body shower before leaving the animal facility.
Treat used animal facility clothing and other waste, including gloves, as contaminated materials and decontaminate before laundering or disposal.

constraints.text: Use extraordinary care to avoid accidental parenteral inoculation with contaminated sharp instruments.
BSL-2 practices, containment equipment, and facilities are recommended for all activities with known or potentially infectious materials from humans and animals.
ABSL-2 practices, containment equipment, and facilities are recommended for animal studies utilizing rodents, armadillos, and NHPs.
Appropriate PPE and containment equipment/facilities are recommended for laboratory work with infective stages of the trematode parasites.

In addition to gloves, long-sleeved laboratory coats and face shields or other protective garb should be worn when working in the immediate area of aquaria or other water sources that may contain schistosome cercariae.

Having squirt bottles that contain 70% ethanol as well as bottles that contain hand sanitizers for which alcohol is the active ingredient strategically placed around the laboratory to facilitate immediate access after accidental spills/exposures.
Ethanol, bleach, and heat can be used to kill snails and cercariae in the water of laboratory aquaria before discharge to sanitary sewers.
Heating the water to ≥50°C will kill the cercariae within seconds.
All staff who work with large animals should at a minimum receive species-specific training in animal behavior, effective handling practices, and other physical safety precautions.
HVAC system pressure differentials should be designed after a site-specific risk assessment.

steps.text: Prepare bleach decontamination solutions fresh (i.e., <24 h).
Inactivate contaminated materials and toxin waste solutions by incineration, extensive autoclaving, or soaking in a suitable decontamination solution.
Absorb liquid inactivated toxins onto a solid matrix for incineration as hazardous waste.
Dispose of liquid inactivated toxins in the sink, depending on local regulations and policies.
Place all disposable contaminated solid material in secondary containers, and then autoclave and/or dispose of as hazardous waste for incineration.
Decontaminate contaminated or potentially contaminated protective clothing and equipment using suitable chemical methods or autoclave after use.
Cover the spill with dry paper towels or other disposable, absorbent material during clean-up.
Ensure that appropriate PPE is worn during the clean-up.
Apply an appropriate decontamination solution to the spill, beginning at the perimeter and working towards the center.
Allow sufficient contact time for the decontamination solution to completely inactivate the toxin.
Restrict access to the contaminated area until the decontamination is complete.
Absorb the decontaminated toxin onto a solid matrix and discard as hazardous waste for incineration.
Cover the powder spill with damp absorbent paper towels if the spill occurs within the BSC.
Apply the appropriate chemical inactivating agent starting at the perimeter and working toward the center.
Wipe the area after the spill has been treated.

constraints.text: Wear PPE including mask, gloves, safety glasses or goggles, and laboratory coat during clean-up.
Spills involving toxin powder have an increased risk of inhalational exposure.
